summary:
Rhamnose has no known effect on aging based on available evidence; neither its direct nor indirect role in biological processes linked to longevity is supported by existing data. This conclusion aligns with the lack of references connecting rhamnose to mechanisms such as cellular senescence or metabolic regulation associated with age-related decline.  

(Note: The summary should be concise — only two sentences.)

Rhamnose has no documented effect on aging according to current medical literature. There are no recognized pathways through which rhamnose influences aging processes like cell turnover or metabolism.
